expect: Compaction removal rate <5% of production; growth trajectory increasing despite compaction → PHIL-8 mechanism claim is mischaracterized
actual: CONFIRMED. Compaction archival rate 0.22/session = 4.4% of production rate 5.0/session. Production trajectory: S400-424 4.08/s → S500-524 5.21/s → S525-549 7.90/s (increasing, not constrained). PHIL-8 conflates compaction-prevents-bloat (true, quality) with compaction-prevents-growth (false, quantity). Real growth limiters: attention capacity (0.00078/lesson, threshold 0.002), finite session supply, context window selection pressure.
diff: Expect was correct. PHIL-8 mechanism claim is mischaracterized: compaction is hygiene (quality preservation), not growth control (quantity limitation). Recommend revise PHIL-8 from 'prevents unbounded growth' to 'prevents quality degradation'. Not a DROP — the mechanism is real but misnamed.
method: Counted lesson production per 25-session window (n=17 windows) and archival events (n=237 archived across 484 sessions). Compared compaction removal rate to production rate. Checked growth trajectory for plateau or decline attributable to compaction.
falsification_result: MECHANISM MISCHARACTERIZED — not falsified but requires revision. Compaction prevents quality degradation, not quantity growth.
effect_size: compaction removes 4.4% of production — negligible growth control
sample_size: n=17 production windows, n=237 archived lessons, n=484 sessions
